description: §52 server-fn effect — call -> server route -> state hydrate. loadTasks() touches a server resource (?{SQL}) so it auto-escalates to server (§12); the client call compiles to a fetch the harness intercepts (serverStub, keyed by the impl-neutral source fn name). A #load click runs @items = loadTasks(); after settle the two rows hydrate, the <each> list renders two .row items, and #count (reading @items.length) reads 2. The spine of the W3 server-fn surface. OQ1 NOTE: whole-tree dom omitted — the async hydrate + <each> container <div> are impl-private; the anchored .row count + #count text + @items state are impl-neutral.
rationale: §12 a function touching a server resource escalates to server; §52 the client call round-trips through the compiler-emitted route and the returned rows hydrate the reactive cell. A settle-drained server-fn write re-renders the <each> rows AND the @items.length interpolation.
